title: The Vulture
content:
The Vulture eats between his meals,
    And that's the reason why
    He very, very, rarely feels
    As well as you and I.

    His eye is dull, his head is bald,
    His neck is growing thinner.
    Oh! what a lesson for us all
    To only eat at dinner!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Hilaire Belloc, titled "The Vulture", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
